name: Framework Prompts

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Framework Prompts is complete and correct

scope:
Author behavioral prompts for all framework classes. Each file goes under a language subdirectory in internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/ (e.g., coding/typescript/react.md, coding/python/django.md).

Frameworks: typescript/react, typescript/vue, typescript/angular, typescript/nextjs, typescript/svelte, javascript/react, javascript/node, python/django, python/fastapi, python/flask, ruby/rails, ruby/sinatra, java/spring, csharp/dotnet, php/laravel, php/symfony, kotlin/android, swift/ios, dart/flutter, elixir/phoenix, rust/actix, rust/tokio.

Each prompt is 30-60 lines. Framework prompts must NOT repeat language fundamentals (the fallback mechanism handles that). Cover framework-specific patterns, testing approaches, and common pitfalls. Researched and current. ZERO wolfcastle content.
success_criteria: All 22 framework prompt files exist under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/<language>/<framework>.md
Every prompt is 30-60 lines and does not repeat language fundamentals covered by the parent language prompt
Zero wolfcastle-specific content in any prompt file
Each prompt covers framework-specific patterns, testing approaches, and common pitfalls

breadcrumbs.text: Created 4 leaf children: frontend-frameworks (6 tasks: ts/react, ts/vue, ts/angular, ts/nextjs, ts/svelte, js/react), backend-scripting-frameworks (6 tasks: js/node, py/django, py/fastapi, py/flask, rb/rails, rb/sinatra), enterprise-frameworks (5 tasks: java/spring, csharp/dotnet, php/laravel, php/symfony, elixir/phoenix), mobile-and-systems-frameworks (5 tasks: kotlin/android, swift/ios, dart/flutter, rust/actix, rust/tokio). Ordering: frontend first (most common framework usage), then backend scripting (natural progression), then enterprise (heavier frameworks), then mobile/systems (most specialized). All 22 frameworks covered. Relevant spec: 2026-03-15T00-04Z-task-classes.md. Relevant ADR: one-level-hierarchical-fallback.
